OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS FILED BY PLAINTIFF WONG, JASON AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (FIRST, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION) IS DENIED. WHEN CONSIDERED AS A WHOLE AND READ IN THE LIGHT MOST FAVORABLE TO PLAINTIFF, THE FIRST, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REFERENCE TO "DEFENDANT" BARBARA JIANG IN PARAGRAPH 16 OF THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED AND FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

SUPPLEMENTAL BALLARD DEC IN SUPPORT OF MTN TO AMEND ANSWER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT WONG, GORDON AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GORDON WONG'S MOTION TO AMEND ANSWER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE HIS AMENDED ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. COURTS SHOULD EXERCISE LIBERALITY IN PERMITTING AMENDMENTS TO A PLEADING, PARTICULARLY IN ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO ANSWERS, "FOR A DEFENDANT DENIED LEAVE TO AMEND IS PERMANENTLY DEPRIVED OF A DEFENSE." (ROYAL THRIFT AND LOAN CO. V. COUNTY ESCROW, INC. (2004) 123 CAL.APP.4TH 24, 41-42.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED HE WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THIS AMENDMENT. THE TRIAL DATE IS NOT UNTIL JUNE 2, 2014. THERE IS STILL SUFFICIENT TIME FOR DISCOVERY PERTAINING TO THE AMENDED ANSWER TO BE PROPOUNDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Appearances:
Conor D. Mack, Esq. of Luscutoff Lendormy & Associates, Tel. 415.989.7500, for defendant and moving party;
Scott L. Woodall, Esq. of the Law Offices of Scott L. Woodall, Tel. 925.600.0111, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed. At the conclusion of the hearing, DEFENDANT BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions is granted in part. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn To Amend Answer To Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: UNIDENTIFIED COUNSEL, FOR PLAINTIFF JASON WONG.
BENJAMIN BALLARD, ESQ., 415.781.3500, FOR DEFENDANT GORDON WONG.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT GORDON WONG's Motion To Amend Answer To Third Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant shall file his amended answer within 10 days of entry of this Order. Courts should exercise liberality in permitting amendments to a pleading, particularly in allowing amendments to answers, "for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense." (Royal Thrift and Loan Co. v. County Escrow, Inc. (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 24, 41-42.) Plaintiff has not established he will be prejudiced by this amendment. The trial date is not until June 2, 2014. There is still sufficient time for discovery pertaining to the amended answer to be propounded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (First, Third and Fourth Causes of Action) is denied. When considered as a whole and read in the light most favorable to Plaintiff, the first, third and fourth causes of action in the Third Amended Complaint are sufficiently pled. The motion to strike the reference to "Defendant" Barbara Jiang in paragraph 16 of the Third Amended Complaint is granted as unopposed and for good cause shown. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
